title: The French love their public libraries. But lack of resources undermines the experience
subhead: France's unique network of 15,500 local cultural spaces, which are open to all, free of charge, has seen visitor numbers steadily increase, leading to certain issues.

As Mali's junta struggles, Algeria regains some of its influence in the Sahel
subhead: Setbacks suffered by the Bamako junta against insurgent groups have reestablished Algiers as a mediator after years of waning influence in the region.

title: Marlène Laruelle: 'Orban's defeat is a particularly strategic loss for Russia'
subhead: The setback for the former Hungarian prime minister, who fueled the Kremlin's narrative that Europe is reluctant to support Ukraine, weakens Vladimir Putin's plans, even though the battle against illiberalism is far from won, explains the historian and political scientist in an interview with Le Monde.

title: 'Severance' star Adam Scott still feels he is 'scrounging for acceptance'
subhead: After breaking out with 'Parks and Recreation,' the 53-year-old American actor has established himself as a major figure on television. At Canneseries, where he gave a master class, he received the Canal+ Icon Award from the festival.

title: The Elysée's smoking room, a masterpiece of 1970s design, rises from the ashes
subhead: When then-president Georges Pompidou entered the Elysée Palace in 1969, he commissioned designer Pierre Paulin to furnish several of the palace's rooms, including a recently-restored smoking room, which is set to go on display soon.

China, the other land of foie gras
subhead: As the French foie gras industry recovers after years of hardship caused by the avian flu epidemic, China has, over the past two years, become the world's second-largest producer of foie gras, writes Le Monde's Laurence Girard in her column.

title: Bologna, a symbol of progressive Italy, is targeted by the far right
subhead: Heir to humanist ideas and an antifascist culture established after World War II, the capital of Emilia-Romagna has become a laboratory for resistance to reactionary ideas in Italy.

title: How Le Monde's London correspondent almost became a Reform UK candidate in Britain's local elections
subhead: After being approached multiple times, Le Monde's London correspondent recounts the questionable recruitment tactics of the far-right party. Her misadventure reflects Reform UK's efforts to try to take London from Labour on May 7.
